data.sentence: Alright, I think we're all on now — Thomas, can you hear us okay?
Yeah, loud and clear. Good to go.
Great, great. So, Thomas, thanks for making time today — I know February is always kind of a weird month for healthcare IT, end of quarter planning and all that.
Ha, yeah, you could say that. We've got our annual risk assessment coming up in March so the timing is actually... it's fine, this is relevant for us.

Oh good, I'm glad to hear that. And I'll just introduce Lisa here — she's been on your account for a few months now and she's really the one who flagged that we should get this session on the calendar.
Hi Thomas, yeah we've exchanged a few emails but I don't think we've actually spoken live before so it's nice to properly connect.
Same, yeah. Good to put a voice to the name.

So Thomas, the reason we wanted to set this up — and feel free to just, you know, kind of take it wherever you want — is we've been collecting feature feedback from customers in the healthcare vertical specifically around Aegis Comply, and your name actually came up a couple times when our team was going through support tickets and conversations.
Yeah, no, that makes sense. Our team has been using Comply for, I want to say, about eight months now? And we have some thoughts.
That's exactly what we want to hear — good, bad, in between. We're not here to pitch anything, this is genuinely a feedback session.

Okay, well — so the main thing, and this has been a recurring pain point for us, is around HIPAA reporting specifically. The current reporting in Comply is... it's okay, but it's pretty static. Like we can pull the report but there's no real way to drill down into specific control areas without basically exporting everything and doing it manually in Excel.
Right, okay. Can you walk me through what that looks like in practice? Like what's the actual workflow that's breaking down?

Sure. So, when our compliance officer — her name's Priya — when she needs to prep for an audit, she's going into Comply, pulling the HIPAA report, and it gives her this flat document basically. But if the auditor comes back and says hey, we want to look specifically at, um, access controls or encryption standards, she has to go back through the whole thing and find those sections manually. There's no way to filter or generate a focused view on a specific control family.
Got it, yeah. So it's more of a navigation and filtering issue within the report output rather than the data itself being wrong.

Correct. The data is — I mean, mostly accurate, we've had a couple discrepancies but nothing major. It's more that the format isn't practical for how audits actually work. Auditors don't look at everything at once, they come in with specific questions.

That's a really useful distinction, thank you. And I want to make sure I'm capturing this right — is this specific to HIPAA or are you seeing the same issue with any other frameworks you're running reports against?
Honestly we're primarily on HIPAA and SOC 2. SOC 2 has the same problem but HIPAA is where it hurts more because the audits are more frequent and the stakes are higher, obviously.

Makes sense. And I should mention — and I don't want to get too far ahead of myself here — but we do have a Comply update in the pipeline and on-demand reporting with more granular control filtering is something that's been discussed internally. I can't give you a hard date today but it's on the roadmap.
Okay. I mean, I've heard 'on the roadmap' before so I'll believe it when I see it, but — that's good to know.
Fair enough, totally fair. We'll follow up with something more concrete once we have it.

The other thing I wanted to flag — and this is maybe more of a workflow request than a feature request — is around how the reports are shared internally. Right now whoever pulls the report kind of has to manually distribute it. Is there any plan for scheduled report delivery? Like email distribution to specific stakeholders on a cadence?
Scheduled delivery, like automated sends — yeah, that's something I've actually heard from a few other customers too. Lisa, do you know if that's been formally submitted to product?
I believe it has been submitted, yeah. I'll double-check after this call and I can send you what I find, Thomas. It may already be tracking somewhere.

That'd be helpful. Priya has been asking about that specifically — she wants to be able to set it and forget it basically, have the monthly compliance summary go to our CISO and our legal team without her having to remember to pull and send it.
Yeah, that's a completely reasonable ask. The manual overhead on something like that adds up fast especially when you're already stretched thin.

Exactly. And one more thing — and this is kind of related — when we generate a HIPAA report right now, there's no executive summary section. Like, the CISO doesn't want to read thirty pages of controls, she wants a one-page summary of where we stand, what the gaps are, and whether anything has changed since last month. Is that something that's been considered?
An executive summary layer, yeah. That's — honestly that's a really specific and useful piece of feedback. Is the gap tracking piece important too, like showing delta from a previous report period?

Yes, absolutely. That's actually more important than the summary itself in some ways. Right now we have no way to tell from within the tool whether our posture has improved or degraded month over month. We're tracking that manually in a spreadsheet which is... not ideal.

Yeah, I can imagine. Okay — so to kind of recap what I'm hearing: granular filtering by control family within reports, scheduled automated delivery to stakeholders, an executive summary layer, and period-over-period delta tracking. Did I get that right?
Yeah, that's the list. I'd rank them in that order of priority too if that helps.
Super helpful, yes. Priority ranking is actually really useful for our product team when they're scoping things.
I do want to ask — are you evaluating any other compliance tools? Just so we have a sense of the landscape from your perspective.

We've looked at a couple things. VaultEdge came up in a conversation internally but nothing serious — we're pretty invested in the Aegis platform at this point and we're not looking to rip and replace. But, you know, if the gaps in Comply don't get addressed, it's a conversation we'd have to have eventually.
Understood, and I appreciate you being direct about that. That's exactly the kind of context that helps us make the case internally for prioritization.

I'll also just say — our experience with the platform overall has been solid. Protect has been reliable for us, Identity is working well. Comply is really the one area where we feel like it's a little behind.
Good to hear on Protect and Identity, and noted on Comply. We'll make sure this feedback gets into the right hands.
So in terms of next steps — Lisa, can you send Thomas a summary of what we captured today and also follow up on the scheduled delivery question?

Yep, I'll send that over by end of this week. Thomas, I'll also loop in our product liaison — her name is Ren — who handles the healthcare vertical. She may want to do a brief follow-up call to get a bit more detail on the control filtering use case specifically.
That's fine. Happy to talk to her. As long as it doesn't turn into a sales call — I've had enough of those this quarter.
Ha — no, I promise, strictly product. Alright Thomas, really appreciate your time today. This was genuinely useful.
Yeah, same. Good conversation. Talk soon.
